model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Florida enters this Sweet 16 matchup as the analytically superior team and the defending national champion, boasting a top-5 defensive efficiency that should provide a higher floor in a high-stakes tournament setting. While Vanderbilt's elite offense and recent 17-point victory over the Gators in the SEC Tournament make them a dangerous opponent, Florida's 16-2 regular-season conference record and their prior 98-94 win over the Commodores demonstrate their ability to win this specific matchup. Expect the Gators' experience and superior interior presence to be the deciding factor as they adjust their defensive schemes to neutralize Vanderbilt's backcourt and secure a spot in the Elite Eight.

reasoningStep.title: South Sweet 16: Florida vs. Vanderbilt
reasoningStep.summary: Florida, the defending national champion and 1-seed, faces SEC rival Vanderbilt in a rubber match after splitting their two previous meetings this season.
evidence: Florida is ranked 4th in KenPom and Torvik with a top-10 offense and top-5 defense.
Vanderbilt is ranked 12th in KenPom with the 6th most efficient offense but a 28th-ranked defense.
Florida won the first meeting 98-94 in January, while Vanderbilt won the SEC Tournament semifinal 91-74 in March.
Florida finished the SEC regular season 16-2 compared to Vanderbilt's 11-7.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
